location.office_time_required: hybrid — NYC office with work-from-home flexibility

secondary_archetype: B2B / Enterprise PM with go-to-market orientation

level_signals: Owns overall product roadmap for StashWorks

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building and scaling B2B products in the employer benefits space.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful experience in B2B employer benefits

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Fintech experience, including familiarity with financial products or regulated environments.
Demonstrated ownership of a product roadmap for a live product with active customers.
Experience supporting sales or go-to-market efforts, including direct customer-facing engagements.

Strong ability to align cross-functional teams and make high-velocity decisions. Engineering is moving faster than ever; you must be able to manage tradeoffs and drive alignment without slowing down the factory.

Proven hands-on experience with AI tools (e.g., Cursor, ChatGPT, etc.) as a core part of daily workflows, driving productivity and enhancing output quality, alongside a commitment to continuously developing AI expertise

Consistent integration of AI into workflows and decision-making, with strong judgment—validating outputs, adhering to Stash guidelines (especially for sensitive data), and owning the quality of AI-assisted work

Deep comfort using modern AI tools (Claude Opus, GPT, Figma AI, etc.) to accelerate your own workflow, analyze customer data, and bridge the gap between idea and execution. You view AI as your co-pilot.
Comfort operating in environments where the product is evolving and processes are still being refined.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience partnering closely with sales, account management, and/or customer success teams
Experience with payroll and/or HCM integrations
Has driven product improvements based on early customer traction and feedback
Has worked at a start-up or high-growth company

scope.surface: StashWorks — B2B employer benefits platform including employer-facing tools, onboarding, integrations, and administrative experiences
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / employer benefits platform within a fintech company
core_responsibilities: Own and drive the product strategy and roadmap for StashWorks
Partner closely with the General Manager during active sales cycles, including customer conversations, demos, and solution shaping
Translate feedback into Agent-ready specs and logic flows; use AI to prototype initial concepts
Lead cross-functional collaboration with engineering, design, compliance, legal, marketing, and customer success
Evolve core B2B functionality such as employer-facing tools, onboarding and implementation flows, integrations, and administrative experiences
Ensure the product meets fintech, compliance, data security, and operational requirements
Use data, customer insights, and qualitative feedback to evaluate performance and guide optimization

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of comprehensive total rewards package

green_flags: Existing product with live customers — not greenfield only
Clear product surface and ownership (StashWorks B2B platform)
Hybrid policy explicitly described for NYC
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Comprehensive benefits package including medical, parental leave, flexible PTO
red_flags: Hybrid role tied to NYC office — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in JD
Salary range is very wide ($73K spread), suggesting significant uncertainty about level
AI tooling requirements are unusually extensive and include specific tools (Claude Opus, Cursor, Figma AI) — may screen out strong PMs with adjacent AI fluency but not exact tool experience
Role title says 'Lead PM' but JD text refers to it as 'Product Manager' — minor inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Lead', comp range, and years required)

ambiguities: Role title in posting header is 'Lead Product Manager' but body text refers to it as 'a Product Manager' — treated as Lead PM per title field.
